approvalHistory.comments: FY2024 Annual Financial Statements submitted for review.
Unqualified audit opinion issued - first in 6 years.
Audit Committee recommends board approval.
Board unanimously approved AFS FY2024.
Denel FY2024 management accounts submitted for AGSA external audit.
Audit fieldwork in progress. Estimated completion March 2025.
FY2025 budget draft compiled and submitted to CFO.
CFO approved for EXCO review with minor adjustments.
EXCO endorsed budget. Risk provisions increased.
FY2025 budget approved unanimously.
Emergency diesel procurement for OCGTs. 3 quotes obtained.
Budget available under emergency allocation.
Approved R4.5bn diesel procurement under emergency provisions.
Board ratified emergency procurement as per resolution BOD-2023-001.
Transnet Recovery Plan 2024-2027 drafted and submitted.
EXCO approved with minor amendments to port KPIs.
Board approved Recovery Plan for publication.
Submitted to DPE and National Treasury as required.
SAA FY2024 AFS submitted. Improved loss position R800m vs R1.5bn prior year.
Qualified opinion with going concern emphasis. Improvement noted on prior year.
Audit Committee accepted AFS with noted conditions.
AFS approved for submission to DPE.
King IV annual compliance assessment completed.
Legal confirmed 94% King IV compliance. 3 areas with partial compliance noted.
BARCO accepted compliance report with improvement actions.
King IV compliance report ratified and included in Annual Report.
Debt relief acceptance resolution drafted.
PFMA compliance confirmed. Resolution legally sound.
Resolution passed unanimously - 7 in favour, 0 against.
Durban Port crane maintenance contract renewal. 4 bids received.
Budget available. BEE scorecard of winning bidder verified.
Approved. Contract value R1.8bn over 3 years.
Board ratified as PFMA Section 54 transaction.
Fleet rejuvenation business case for 10 A320neos compiled.
Financial modelling verified. NPV positive at base case.
EXCO endorsed for Board submission.
Board approved for DPE PFMA Section 54 submission.
PFMA Section 54 approval granted. Proceed with procurement.
Transnet FY2024 AFS submitted for KPMG external audit.
Unqualified opinion with going concern emphasis paragraph.
Audit Committee recommends Board approval.
AFS and Annual Report approved unanimously.
Rooivalk export contract approval resolution and PFMA Section 54 submission prepared.
NCACC compliance confirmed. PFMA process followed correctly.
Passed unanimously.
Ministerial approval granted for contract exceeding R5bn.
Annual environmental compliance report compiled for 15 coal power stations.
Annual report accepted. AEL variances for Tutuka and Duvha noted for follow-up.
Environmental compliance report accepted. Variance plan actions tracked.
Denel FY2024 budget compiled reflecting turnaround plan Phase 2 targets.
CEO approved with Rooivalk contract revenue included.
FY2024 budget approved. DPE performance compact aligned.
Heavy maintenance check (D-check) for 2 A320 aircraft. Quotes from Lufthansa Technik and HAECO.
Lufthansa Technik recommended at R185m per aircraft.
Budget available. Contract total R370m approved.
CEO ratified. Contract executed.
FY2023 AFS submitted for AGSA external audit.
Qualified opinion issued. Material irregular expenditure R4.2bn. Revenue recognition matter.
Management letter of response submitted to AGSA. Action plan tabled.
AFS accepted with qualified opinion noted. Annual Report to reflect qualification.
Denel Turnaround Plan 2022-2026 compiled with external advisors.
Financial projections validated. Operational milestones achievable.
EXCO endorsed with 5 amendments.
Formally adopted. DPE and Treasury notified.
IOSA self-assessment completed. 98.4% compliance with applicable standards.
IOSA audit completed. 4 minor observations raised. All closed during audit.
SACAA notified of successful IOSA renewal. AOC confirmed current.
CFO recommends R1.8bn final dividend based on FY2024 results and cash position.
Dividend resolution passed 6-1. One director abstained on basis of capital investment needs.
New coal supply agreement with Exxaro for Matimba and Medupi. 10-year supply 5Mtpa.
Contract terms legally reviewed. BEE compliance verified at Level 2.
10-year contract value R42bn approved.
Board approved per PFMA Section 54 requirements.
FY2020 AFS submitted.
Unqualified opinion issued. Going concern emphasis paragraph included.
FY2020 AFS approved.
MYPD6 tariff application compiled. 5-year period FY2025/26-FY2029/30.
Financial data verified. Legal compliance confirmed.
MYPD6 application approved for NERSA submission.
Application submitted to NERSA on due date.
LMT partnership agreement PFMA Section 54 submission prepared.
Financial terms and value verified. Section 54 threshold exceeded.
Submission to Minister of Public Enterprises.
Section 54 ministerial approval granted.
FY2024 operating and capital budget compiled.
Budget approved for EXCO review.
EXCO endorsed with NDP capital allocation adjustments.
FY2024 budget approved.
FY2022 AFS - first post-business rescue annual financial statements.
Qualified opinion. Going concern, negative equity, ongoing recapitalisation uncertainty.
AFS accepted noting qualified opinion. Relaunch progress positive.
Q4 FY2024 preliminary results reviewed and approved for board consideration.
Audit Committee reviewed results. No concerns noted for preliminary figures.
Board approved final dividend of KES 4.1bn for FY2023.
National Treasury confirmed receipt and acceptance of dividend declaration.
Strategic Plan approved by KPLC management.
Board formally adopted KPLC Strategic Plan 2023-2027.
Navis N4 TOS evaluated as best value — technical score 88/100, financial score 92/100.
Contract terms reviewed by legal team. No issues identified.
Contract awarded to Navis N4 for PCS Phase 2 at KES 1.8bn.
SCAC FY2024 annual returns workflow initiated. Deadline 30 September 2024.
Fuel hedging coverage gap escalated from Audit & Risk Committee to full Board per escalation policy.
Board approved disposal of B777-300ER aircraft 5Y-KZD and 5Y-KZE.
Asset disposal tender launched — RFP sent to 5 aircraft dealers.
5Y-KZD sold for USD 34m and 5Y-KZE for USD 34m. Total proceeds USD 68m applied to debt reduction.
Code-share settlement revenue leakage risk acknowledged. KES 320m estimated exposure.
Remediation plan submitted to Audit Committee — 45-day implementation timeline.
Berths 25-26 business case reviewed — NPV positive at KES 45bn over 30 years.
Board approved Berths 25-26 project at KES 28bn budget — Resolution KPAB/198/003.
FY2023 KPLC annual financial statements reviewed and approved by CFO.
Audit Committee reviewed and approved annual financial statements.
Board formally adopted FY2023 Annual Report and Financial Statements.
Ministry confirmed acceptance of KPLC FY2023 Annual Report.
Tariff review submission reviewed internally. Navigant Consulting advisory incorporated.
Board approved tariff review submission. Filed with EPRA 31 January 2024.
EPRA acknowledged receipt of tariff review submission ref. EPRA/TRF/2024/001.
KQ Restructuring Plan 2023-2026 reviewed by management.
Finance Committee endorsed plan financials.
Board adopted Kenya Airways Restructuring Plan 2023-2026.
Mwongozo self-assessment questionnaire completed for KQ.
Board chair reviewed and endorsed self-assessment results.
Mwongozo assessment submitted to PS Transport. Score 72%.
FY2024/25 performance targets reviewed and agreed by KPA management.
Board endorsed Performance Contract targets at 199th meeting.
FY2024/25 Performance Contract signed by CS Transport and KPA MD.
Board approved LMCP Phase III Phase 2 KES 9.5bn budget.
World Bank issued no-objection for procurement plan. Phase III disbursement approved.
KPA FY2023 annual financial statements reviewed by CFO.
PwC issued clean audit opinion on KPA FY2023 statements.
Board adopted FY2023 Annual Report and Financial Statements.
Ministry accepted KPA FY2023 Annual Report.
KPA Mwongozo annual self-assessment commenced.
KPA board chair endorsed self-assessment score of 87%.
KPA Mwongozo assessment submitted to PS Transport — top-rated amongst Tier 1 SOEs.
Recruitment of 15 B787 pilots approved following Air Lease Corporation B787 delivery schedule.
Board approved recruitment budget of KES 85m for new pilot recruitment.
12 B787 type-rated pilots recruited — 8 internal transitions, 4 external hires.
KQ SMS Manual updated per KCAA audit findings.
8 KCAA SMS audit findings closed.
KCAA issued closure certificate for all 8 SMS audit findings.
KPLC internal audit raised 12 high-priority findings including CIS cybersecurity vulnerability.
Management provided remediation plan — 60 days to close all 12 high-priority findings.
Digital transformation programme business case reviewed — ROI 35% over 5 years.
IT Committee endorsed digital transformation programme including ERP and customer portal.
Board approved KES 2.8bn digital transformation budget for FY2025.
KQ ESG Report 2023 drafted and reviewed by Sustainability team.
Sustainability Committee approved ESG Report for publication.
CEO signed off KQ ESG Report 2023 for publication.
ESG Framework directive acknowledged. Gap analysis against GRI standards commenced.
KQ FY2023 annual financial statements reviewed — net loss KES 3.8bn noted with improvement from FY2022.
EY issued modified audit opinion with emphasis of matter on going concern.
Board adopted FY2023 annual results.
Ministry accepted KQ FY2023 Annual Report with noted concerns on going concern.
Navigant Consulting evaluated as best regulatory advisory firm for EPRA tariff review.
Board approved Navigant appointment at special board meeting.
Contract with Navigant Consulting signed — KES 85m for tariff review advisory services.
